title: pteryxia terebinthina var. calcarea
intro: Pteryxia terebinthina var. calcarea, also known as calcareous wormwood, is a variant of the Pteryxia terebinthina plant, which belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This perennial herb is native to the western coast of North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Calcareous wormwood can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and its herbal preparations have been used to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
